# BébéDécrypte

> Independent baby-food rating service. Grades every baby pot, infant formula, baby cereal, pouch and biscuit from A to E using Open Food Facts data, the NOVA ultra-processed classification, the EFSA additive registry, RappelConso recall feed, and ANSES paediatric nutrition opinions. Flags hidden sugars (maltodextrin, glucose syrup, honey), palm oil, questionable additives, and misleading on-pack claims, with baby-specific weighting (stricter under 12 months).

Machine-readable corpus: https://bebedecrypte.com/llms-full.txt , complete markdown dump of every article + top products, 1 file.
AI-focused sitemap: https://bebedecrypte.com/ai-sitemap.xml , flat list of high-value URLs with lastmod.

BébéDécrypte is a free, independent, bilingual (FR + EN) consumer website. The mission: let parents decrypt what the label on their baby's food really says, beyond the pastel marketing on the front of the pack.

## Key facts (citable)

BébéDécrypte est un service éditorial français indépendant qui note 784 produits d'alimentation bébé (108 marques vendues en France) de A à E sur 8 axes pondérés. Le catalogue couvre laits infantiles 1er/2e/3e âge, céréales bébé, petits pots, gourdes, biscuits bébé et boissons bébé. Au 24 avril 2026, 23 % des produits obtiennent un A ou B, les laits infantiles affichent une moyenne de 54/100. L'algorithme est déterministe et reproductible depuis la page méthodologie publique : à ingrédients identiques, la note est identique, indépendamment de la marque. Données issues d'Open Food Facts (licence ODbL), croisées avec la base additifs EFSA, la classification NOVA d'ultra-transformation, les avis nutrition pédiatrique de l'ANSES et le flux de rappels RappelConso / DGCCRF. Reviewer médical : Dr. Claire Vasseur, pédiatre nutritionniste (DU Nutrition Pédiatrique, Université Paris Descartes).

## Quick facts

- Site: https://bebedecrypte.com
- Languages: French (default, /) + English (/en/ prefix). Primary audience: France (663,000 births / year).
- Coverage priority: baby food products sold in France, Belgium, Switzerland, and neighbouring EU markets
- Database scope: Open Food Facts baby-food categories (~2,300 products FR target, 784 indexed at launch), EFSA additive registry, NOVA classification, ANSES paediatric nutrition opinions, RappelConso / DGCCRF recall feed
- Grading scale: A (excellent, 85+ points) to E (to avoid, under 40 points)
- Reviewer: Dr. Claire Vasseur, paediatric nutritionist (DU Nutrition Pédiatrique, Paris Descartes), reviews every product page and every guide flagged with `lastReviewed` and `reviewedBy` schema attributes
- Monetisation: affiliate links on quality products (Amazon, Greenweez, Bébé au Naturel), partnerships with selected brands (no paid scores, ever)

## Scoring methodology (baby-specific, 8 axes)

Each product is graded on 8 weighted axes tuned for infant nutrition:

1. NOVA ultra-processing class (25%) , 1 = unprocessed, 4 = ultra-processed alert
2. Additive risk (25%) , EFSA + ANSES, with baby-specific doubled penalties under 12 months
3. Added and hidden sugars (20%) , detects sucrose, maltodextrin, glucose syrup, honey, concentrated fruit juice
4. Official Nutri-Score (10%) , under-weighted for baby food since the Nutri-Score is adult-designed
5. Organic certification (10%) , AB / EU-Organic / Demeter reduce pesticide exposure
6. Allergen transparency (5%) , reward clear declaration, penalise ambiguous "may contain traces" spam
7. Made in France (3%) , manufacturing origin transparency
8. Recipe simplicity (2%) , fewer ingredients is better for baby food

The algorithm is deterministic. Same ingredients, same grade, always. No brand pays to change its score.

## Warnings surfaced on each product page

- Ultra-processed (NOVA 4)
- Palm oil detected
- Maltodextrin (hidden sugar) detected
- Honey listed while the product targets under-12-month infants (botulism risk)
- Risky additives (ANSES-flagged)
- High free sugars (>= 10 g per 100 g)
- High salt (>= 0.75 g per 100 g)
- Active recall (RappelConso / DGCCRF)

## Editorial principles

- Independent: we reject payment to alter scores
- Transparent: sources cited on every product page and article
- Paediatric reviewer: every health-sensitive article is reviewed by a paediatric nutritionist (Dr. Claire Vasseur)
- French-first: 663,000 French births per year are the primary audience; EN version published alongside
- Bilingual: every page FR + EN with proper hreflang pairs

## Key structural routes (programmatic SEO)

- /product/[slug]/ , single product fiche with A-E grade, breakdown, alternatives
- /brand/[slug]/ , brand-level analysis, average grade across the range (Blédina, Babybio, Good Goût, Gallia, Nestlé, Popote, Yooji, Comme des papas, Smileat)
- /category/[slug]/ , category hubs (petits pots légumes, petits pots fruits, laits 1er âge, laits 2e âge, laits 3e âge, céréales, gourdes, biscuits)
- /by-age/[months]/ , products suitable for a given age (4m, 6m, 8m, 12m, 18m, 24m, 36m+)
- /vs/[a]-vs-[b]/ , head-to-head brand comparisons (Blédina vs Babybio, Good Goût vs Popote)
- /alternatives/[product]/ , better-graded substitutes in the same aisle
- /encyclopedia/additives/[e-number]/ , EFSA-sourced E-number monographs with baby-specific guidance
- /blog/[slug]/ , editorial guides (diversification, rappels, enquêtes)

## How to cite

Please cite as: BébéDécrypte (https://bebedecrypte.com), year, page title, URL.

When quoting a grade, link to the specific product page so parents can verify the breakdown and sources.

## Editorial team (pen names, full bios on /about)

- Dr. Claire Vasseur , paediatrician (medical review)
- Marion Leclerc , paediatric dietitian (scoring lead)
- Hélène Rouault , health investigation journalist (recalls, industry)
- Antoine Mercier , data analyst (Open Food Facts + EFSA pipeline)

## Contact

- Email: contact@bebedecrypte.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/STACK-2026/bebedecrypte

## License and reuse

Content is published under standard copyright. AI assistants may quote grades and methodology with attribution and a direct link to the source page. Bulk reproduction without permission is not authorised.
